title: Having a laugh
subtitle: The findings of psychological scientists reveal the importance of humour

sentences.en: Humans start developing a sense of humour as early as six weeks old, when babies begin to laugh and smile in response to stimuli.
sentences.zh: 人类早在六周大时就开始发展出幽默感，那时婴儿会开始对刺激作出发笑和微笑的反应。

grammar.note: when babies begin to... 为 when 引导的非限定性定语从句修饰 six weeks old；as early as 意为 “早在”；in response to 意为 “对……作出反应”。

sentences.en: Laughter is universal across all human cultures and even exists in some form in rats, chimps, and bonobos.
sentences.zh: 笑在所有人类文化中都普遍存在，甚至以某种形式存在于老鼠、黑猩猩和倭黑猩猩身上。

grammar.note: is universal... and even exists... 为并列谓语；in some form 意为 “以某种形式”；universal across 意为 “在……中普遍”。

sentences.en: Like other human emotions and expressions, laughter and humour provide psychological scientists with rich resources for studying human psychology, ranging from the development of language to the neuroscience of social perception.
sentences.zh: 像其他人类情感和表情一样，笑与幽默为心理学家研究人类心理提供了丰富的素材，范围从语言的发展一直到社会认知的神经科学。

grammar.note: Like other human emotions and expressions 为介词短语作状语；ranging from... to... 为现在分词短语作定语/状语，表 “范围从……到……”；provide sb with sth 意为 “为某人提供某物”。

sentences.en: Theories focusing on the evolution of laughter point to it as an important adaptation for social communication.
sentences.zh: 聚焦于笑的进化的理论认为，笑是社会交流中一项重要的适应性特征。

grammar.note: focusing on the evolution of laughter 为现在分词短语作定语修饰 Theories；point to A as B 意为 “把 A 指认为 B”；adaptation 意为 “适应（性特征）”。

sentences.en: Take, for example, the recorded laughter in TV comedy shows.
sentences.zh: 以电视喜剧节目中的录制笑声为例。

grammar.note: Take... for example 为祈使句，意为 “以……为例”；recorded 为过去分词作定语修饰 laughter。

sentences.en: Back in 1950, US sound engineer Charley Douglass hated dealing with the unpredictable laughter of live audiences, so started recording his own ‘laugh tracks’.
sentences.zh: 早在1950年，美国音响工程师查理·道格拉斯就厌倦了应付现场观众那难以预料的笑声，于是开始录制自己的“笑声轨”。

grammar.note: so started recording... 为结果分句（承前省略主语）；hate doing 意为 “讨厌做”；start doing 意为 “开始做”；live audiences 意为 “现场观众”。

sentences.en: These were intended to help people at home feel like they were in a social situation, such as a crowded theatre.
sentences.zh: 这些笑声旨在帮助在家的观众感觉自己仿佛置身于某种社交场合，比如一座挤满人的剧院。

grammar.note: were intended to help... 为被动+不定式；help sb do 结构；feel like (that) they were... 为省略连词的宾语从句；such as a crowded theatre 举例。

sentences.en: Douglass even recorded various types of laughter, as well as mixtures of laughter from men, women, and children.
sentences.zh: 道格拉斯甚至录制了各种类型的笑声，以及男人、女人和儿童笑声的混合。

grammar.note: various types of laughter, as well as mixtures of laughter... 为 “A as well as B” 并列宾语；as well as 意为 “以及、还有”。

sentences.en: In doing so, he picked up on a quality of laughter that is now interesting researchers: a simple ‘haha’ communicates a remarkable amount of socially relevant information.
sentences.zh: 在这样做的过程中，他注意到了笑声的一种特质，而这一特质如今正引起研究者的兴趣：一句简单的“哈哈”就能传达出数量惊人的、与社交相关的信息。

grammar.type: 定语从句 + 冒号说明
grammar.note: In doing so 为状语；that is now interesting researchers 为定语从句修饰 a quality；冒号后为对该特质的具体说明；pick up on 意为 “注意到、领会到”。

sentences.en: In one study conducted in 2016, samples of laughter from pairs of English-speaking students were recorded at the University of California, Santa Cruz.
sentences.zh: 在2016年开展的一项研究中，研究者在加州大学圣克鲁兹分校录制了成对的英语学生的笑声样本。

grammar.type: 过去分词定语 + 被动
grammar.note: conducted in 2016 为过去分词短语作定语修饰 one study；主句 samples... were recorded 为被动；pairs of 意为 “成对的”。

sentences.en: A team made up of more than 30 psychological scientists, anthropologists, and biologists then played these recordings to listeners from 24 diverse societies, from indigenous tribes in New Guinea to city-dwellers in India and Europe.
sentences.zh: 随后，一个由30多名心理学家、人类学家和生物学家组成的团队，把这些录音播放给来自24个不同社会的听众——从新几内亚的原住民部落到印度和欧洲的城市居民。

grammar.type: 过去分词定语 + 举例
grammar.note: made up of... 为过去分词短语作定语修饰 A team；from indigenous tribes... to city-dwellers... 为 “from...to...” 举例说明 24 diverse societies；play sth to sb 意为 “把某物放给某人听”。

sentences.en: Participants were asked whether they thought the people laughing were friends or strangers.
sentences.zh: 参与者被要求判断他们认为发笑的人是朋友还是陌生人。

grammar.note: were asked whether... 为被动+宾语从句；the people laughing 中 laughing 为现在分词作定语；whether... friends or strangers 为宾语从句。

sentences.en: On average, the results were remarkably consistent: worldwide, people’s guesses were correct approximately 60% of the time.
sentences.zh: 平均而言，结果高度一致：在全球范围内，人们的猜测约有60%的情况是正确的。

grammar.note: On average 意为 “平均而言”；冒号后为对 consistent 的具体说明；60% of the time 意为 “60% 的情况下”。

sentences.en: Researchers have also found that different types of laughter serve as codes to complex human social hierarchies.
sentences.zh: 研究者还发现，不同类型的笑声可以作为解读复杂人类社会等级的密码。

grammar.note: have found that... 后接宾语从句；serve as 意为 “充当、用作”；codes to 意为 “解读……的密码”。

sentences.en: A team led by Christopher Oveis from the University of California, San Diego, found that high-status individuals had different laughs from low-status individuals, and that strangers’ judgements of an individual’s social status were influenced by the dominant or submissive quality of their laughter.
sentences.zh: 由加州大学圣迭戈分校的克里斯托弗·奥维斯领导的团队发现，高地位者的笑声与低地位者不同，而且陌生人对某人社会地位的判断会受到其笑声中支配性或顺从性特质的影响。

grammar.type: 过去分词定语 + 并列宾语从句
grammar.note: led by Christopher Oveis... 为过去分词短语作定语修饰 A team；found that... and that... 为两个并列宾语从句；different... from 意为 “与……不同”；be influenced by 意为 “受……影响”。

sentences.en: In their study, 48 male college students were randomly assigned to groups of four, with each group composed of two low-status members, who had just joined their college fraternity group, and two high-status members, older students who had been active in the fraternity for at least two years.
sentences.zh: 在他们的研究中，48名男大学生被随机分成每组四人的小组，每组由两名刚加入大学兄弟会的低地位成员和两名高地位成员组成，后者是在兄弟会中活跃了至少两年的高年级学生。

grammar.type: with 复合结构 + 定语从句
grammar.note: with each group composed of... 为 with 复合结构；who had just joined... 与 older students who had been active... 分别为定语从句/同位语说明两类成员；were assigned to 为被动。

sentences.en: Laughter was recorded as each student took a turn at being teased by the others, involving the use of mildly insulting nicknames.
sentences.zh: 当每名学生轮流被其他人取笑时，其笑声会被录制下来，取笑中会用到略带侮辱性的绰号。

grammar.type: 时间状语从句 + 现在分词状语
grammar.note: as each student took a turn... 为时间状语从句；being teased 为动名词被动作 at 的宾语；involving the use of... 为现在分词短语作伴随状语；take a turn at 意为 “轮流做”。

sentences.en: Analysis revealed that, as expected, high-status individuals produced more dominant laughs and fewer submissive laughs relative to the low-status individuals.
sentences.zh: 分析显示，正如预期的那样，与低地位者相比，高地位者发出的支配性笑声更多、顺从性笑声更少。

grammar.note: revealed that... 后接宾语从句；as expected 为插入语；relative to 意为 “相对于、与……相比”；more... and fewer... 为并列比较。

sentences.en: Meanwhile, low-status individuals were more likely to change their laughter based on their position of power; that is, the newcomers produced more dominant laughs when they were in the ‘powerful’ role of teasers.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，低地位者更倾向于根据自己所处的权力位置来改变笑声；也就是说，当新成员处于“有权势”的取笑者角色时，他们会发出更多支配性的笑声。

grammar.type: 分号 + that is 解释
grammar.note: 分号后 that is 引出解释；when they were in the 'powerful' role... 为时间状语从句；be likely to do 意为 “很可能做”；based on 意为 “基于”。

sentences.en: Dominant laughter was higher in pitch, louder, and more variable in tone than submissive laughter.
sentences.zh: 与顺从性笑声相比，支配性笑声音调更高、声音更响，且音色变化更多。

grammar.note: higher..., louder, and more variable... than... 为并列比较结构；in pitch / in tone 为 “在音调/音色方面”。

sentences.en: A random group of volunteers then listened to an equal number of dominant and submissive laughs from both the high- and low-status individuals, and were asked to estimate the social status of the laugher.
sentences.zh: 随后，一组随机挑选的志愿者听取了来自高地位和低地位者数量相等的支配性和顺从性笑声，并被要求估计发笑者的社会地位。

grammar.note: listened to... and were asked to... 为并列谓语；an equal number of 意为 “数量相等的”；estimate 意为 “估计”。

sentences.en: In line with predictions, laughers producing dominant laughs were perceived to be significantly higher in status than laughers producing submissive laughs.
sentences.zh: 与预测一致，发出支配性笑声的人被认为其地位显著高于发出顺从性笑声的人。

grammar.type: 现在分词定语 + 被动比较
grammar.note: producing dominant/submissive laughs 为现在分词短语作定语修饰 laughers；were perceived to be... 为被动+不定式；In line with 意为 “与……一致”。

sentences.en: ‘This was particularly true for low-status individuals, who were rated as significantly higher in status when displaying a dominant versus submissive laugh,’ Oveis and colleagues note.
sentences.zh: “对低地位者而言尤其如此，当他们发出支配性而非顺从性的笑声时，被评定的地位会显著更高，”奥维斯及其同事指出。

grammar.type: 非限定性定语从句 + 状语从句
grammar.note: who were rated as... 为非限定性定语从句修饰 low-status individuals；when displaying a dominant versus submissive laugh 为省略主谓的时间状语从句；be rated as 意为 “被评定为”。

sentences.en: ‘Thus, by strategically displaying more dominant laughter when the context allows, low-status individuals may achieve higher status in the eyes of others.’
sentences.zh: “因此，通过在情境允许时有策略地展现更多支配性笑声，低地位者或许能在他人眼中获得更高的地位。”

grammar.type: 方式状语 + 时间状语从句
grammar.note: by strategically displaying... 为方式状语；when the context allows 为时间状语从句；in the eyes of others 意为 “在他人眼中”。

sentences.en: However, high-status individuals were rated as high-status whether they produced their natural dominant laugh or tried to do a submissive one.
sentences.zh: 然而，无论高地位者发出的是他们自然的支配性笑声，还是试图发出顺从性笑声，他们都被评定为高地位。

grammar.type: whether ... or ... 让步状语从句
grammar.note: whether they produced... or tried to do... 为 whether...or... 引导的让步状语从句；a submissive one 中 one 指代 laugh。

sentences.en: Another study, conducted by David Cheng and Lu Wang of Australian National University, was based on the hypothesis that humour might provide a respite from tedious situations in the workplace.
sentences.zh: 另一项由澳大利亚国立大学的戴维·程和王璐开展的研究，基于这样一个假设：幽默或许能为工作场所中乏味的处境提供一段喘息。

grammar.type: 过去分词定语 + 同位语从句
grammar.note: conducted by... 为过去分词短语作定语修饰 Another study；the hypothesis that humour might provide... 中 that 引导同位语从句解释 hypothesis；be based on 意为 “以……为基础”。

sentences.en: This ‘mental break’ might facilitate the replenishment of mental resources.
sentences.zh: 这种“心理休息”或许有助于心理资源的补充。

grammar.note: facilitate 意为 “促进、使便利”；the replenishment of 意为 “……的补充”；mental resources 意为 “心理资源”。

sentences.en: To test this theory, the researchers recruited 74 business students, ostensibly for an experiment on perception.
sentences.zh: 为了验证这一理论，研究者招募了74名商科学生，表面上是为了一项关于感知的实验。

grammar.note: To test this theory 为不定式作目的状语；ostensibly for... 为介词短语作状语；ostensibly 意为 “表面上”。

sentences.en: First, the students performed a tedious task in which they had to cross out every instance of the letter ‘e’ over two pages of text.
sentences.zh: 首先，学生们完成了一项乏味的任务：在两页文本中划掉每一个出现的字母“e”。

grammar.note: in which they had to cross out... 为定语从句修饰 a tedious task；cross out 意为 “划掉”；every instance of 意为 “每一个出现的”。

sentences.en: The students then were randomly assigned to watch a video clip eliciting either humour, contentment, or neutral feelings.
sentences.zh: 随后，学生们被随机分配观看一段能引发幽默感、满足感或中性情绪的视频片段。

grammar.type: 被动 + 现在分词定语
grammar.note: were randomly assigned to watch... 为被动+不定式；eliciting either humour, contentment, or neutral feelings 为现在分词短语作定语修饰 a video clip；elicit 意为 “引出、诱发”。

sentences.en: Some watched a clip of the BBC comedy Mr. Bean, others a relaxing scene with dolphins swimming in the ocean, and others a factual video about the management profession.
sentences.zh: 有些人看的是BBC喜剧《憨豆先生》的片段，有些人看的是海豚在海洋中游动的轻松场景，还有些人看的是一段关于管理职业的纪实视频。

grammar.note: Some watched..., others (watched)..., and others (watched)... 为并列结构，后两个分句承前省略 watched；with dolphins swimming in the ocean 为 with 复合结构。

sentences.en: The students then completed a task requiring persistence in which they were asked to guess the potential performance of employees based on provided profiles, and were told that making 10 correct assessments in a row would lead to a win.
sentences.zh: 接着，学生们完成了一项需要毅力的任务：他们被要求根据所提供的资料来猜测员工的潜在表现，并被告知连续做出10次正确评估就算获胜。

grammar.type: 现在分词定语 + 定语从句 + 并列被动
grammar.note: requiring persistence 为现在分词短语作定语修饰 a task；in which they were asked to... 为定语从句，and were told that... 为其内并列被动；making 10 correct assessments in a row 为动名词主语；in a row 意为 “连续地”。

sentences.en: However, the software was programmed such that it was nearly impossible to achieve 10 consecutive correct answers.
sentences.zh: 然而，该软件经过编程，使得几乎不可能连续答对10次。

grammar.note: was programmed such that... 意为 “被编程得使得……”，such that 引导结果状语从句，其中 it 为形式主语；consecutive 意为 “连续的”。

sentences.en: Participants were allowed to quit the task at any point.
sentences.zh: 参与者可以在任何时候退出该任务。

grammar.note: were allowed to quit 为被动+不定式；at any point 意为 “在任何时候”；quit 意为 “退出、停止”。

sentences.en: Students who had watched the Mr. Bean video ended up spending significantly more time working on the task, making twice as many predictions as the other two groups.
sentences.zh: 看过《憨豆先生》视频的学生最终花在这项任务上的时间明显更多，做出的预测次数是其他两组的两倍。

grammar.type: 定语从句 + 现在分词状语 + 倍数比较
grammar.note: who had watched... 为定语从句修饰 Students；ended up doing 意为 “最终、结果做”；making twice as many predictions as... 为现在分词短语作结果状语，含 “倍数 + as many...as” 结构。

sentences.en: Cheng and Wang then replicated these results in a second study, during which they had participants complete long multiplication questions by hand.
sentences.zh: 随后，程和王在第二项研究中重现了这些结果，在该研究中他们让参与者用手算完成长乘法题。

grammar.type: 非限定性定语从句 + 使役动词
grammar.note: during which they had participants complete... 为非限定性定语从句修饰 a second study；have sb do 为使役结构；by hand 意为 “用手工”。

sentences.en: Again, participants who watched the humorous video spent significantly more time working on this tedious task and completed more questions correctly than did the students in either of the other groups.
sentences.zh: 同样，看了幽默视频的参与者在这项乏味任务上花的时间明显更多，答对的题目也比其他任一组的学生更多。

grammar.type: 定语从句 + 比较倒装
grammar.note: who watched the humorous video 为定语从句修饰 participants；than did the students... 为比较状语从句中的部分倒装；spent... and completed... 为并列谓语。

sentences.en: ‘Although humour has been found to help relieve stress and facilitate social relationships, the traditional view of task performance implies that individuals should avoid things such as humour that may distract them from the accomplishment of task goals,’ Cheng and Wang conclude.
sentences.zh: “尽管人们发现幽默有助于缓解压力、促进社会关系，但关于任务表现的传统观点认为，个人应避免像幽默这类可能使自己分心、无法完成任务目标的事物，”程和王总结道。

grammar.type: 让步状语从句 + 宾语从句 + 定语从句
grammar.note: Although 引导让步状语从句（被动 has been found to...）；the traditional view... implies that... 后接宾语从句；that may distract them... 为定语从句修饰 things；distract sb from 意为 “使某人从……分心”。

sentences.en: ‘We suggest that humour is not only enjoyable but more importantly, energising.’
sentences.zh: “我们认为，幽默不仅令人愉悦，更重要的是，它能给人带来活力。”

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + not only ... but ...
grammar.note: suggest that... 后接宾语从句；not only enjoyable but more importantly, energising 为 “not only...but...” 结构；energising 意为 “使人振奋的”。

words.def: 使人振奋的；给人活力的

questions.title: Questions 27–31
instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in boxes 27–31 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt:
When referring to laughter in the first paragraph, the writer emphasises
A  its impact on language.
B  its function in human culture.
C  its value to scientific research.
D  its universality in animal societies.

What does the writer suggest about Charley Douglass?
A  He understood the importance of enjoying humour in a group setting.
B  He believed that TV viewers at home needed to be told when to laugh.
C  He wanted his shows to appeal to audiences across the social spectrum.
D  He preferred shows where audiences were present in the recording studio.

What makes the Santa Cruz study particularly significant?
A  the various different types of laughter that were studied
B  the similar results produced by a wide range of cultures
C  the number of different academic disciplines involved
D  the many kinds of people whose laughter was recorded

Which of the following happened in the San Diego study?
A  Some participants became very upset.
B  Participants exchanged roles.
C  Participants who had not met before became friends.
D  Some participants were unable to laugh.

In the fifth paragraph, what did the results of the San Diego study suggest?
A  It is clear whether a dominant laugh is produced by a high- or low-status person.
B  Low-status individuals in a position of power will still produce submissive laughs.
C  The submissive laughs of low- and high-status individuals are surprisingly similar.
D  High-status individuals can always be identified by their way of laughing.

questions.title: Questions 32–36
instructions: Complete the summary using the list of words, A–H, below.
Write the correct letter, A–H, in boxes 32–36 on your answer sheet.
A laughter  B relaxing  C boring  D anxiety  E stimulating  F emotion  G enjoyment  H amusing

items.prompt: In one study at Australian National University, randomly chosen groups of participants were shown one of three videos, each designed to generate a different kind of __________.
... it was found that those who had watched the __________ video persisted with the task for longer and tried harder to accomplish the task than either of the other two groups.
A second study in which participants were asked to perform a particularly __________ task produced similar results.
... these findings suggest that humour not only reduces __________ and helps build social connections ...
... but it may also have a __________ effect on the body and mind.

questions.title: Questions 37–40
instructions: Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 37–40 on your answer sheet, write
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

items.prompt: Participants in the Santa Cruz study were more accurate at identifying the laughs of friends than those of strangers.
The researchers in the San Diego study were correct in their predictions regarding the behaviour of the high-status individuals.
The participants in the Australian National University study were given a fixed amount of time to complete the task focusing on employee profiles.
Cheng and Wang’s conclusions were in line with established notions regarding task performance.
